sanskrit:
बहिरन्तश्च भूतानामचरं चरमेव च।
सूक्ष्मत्वात्तदविज्ञेयं दूरस्थं चान्तिके च तत्॥
transliteration:
bahir antaś ca bhūtānām acaraṃ caram eva ca |
sūkṣmatvāt tad avijñeyaṃ dūrasthaṃ cāntike ca tat ||
literal_meaning: Outside and inside of all beings, the unmoving and the moving; because of subtlety That is unknowable, far away yet near.
contextual_meaning: The Ground of Being is simultaneously inside and outside every experience, moving and still, too subtle for ordinary perception, yet closer than your next breath.
psychological_state: Awe-based humility that dissolves rigid self-other boundaries; recognition that the most important reality is simultaneously everywhere and nowhere in conscious awareness.
trigger_scenario: You’re stuck in traffic, frustrated, then suddenly notice your own heartbeat and realize the same pulse is in every driver around you—an invisible shared presence you’d overlooked.
anchor_text: The silent awareness that holds every thought, feeling, and breath is nearer than your own heartbeat.
